name: Maylis to Doazit via l'étang de la Hosse – loop
name_original: Maylis à Doazit via l'étang de la Hosse – boucle
region_intro: Hiking around Maylis, a commune in the Landes department of Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, offers routes through a gently undulating landscape. The region is characterized by low elevations, ranging from 43 to 122 meters, suggesting a terrain that is relatively flat rather than mountainous. Trails often traverse rural areas, agricultural lands, and may include small forests or ponds, providing a tranquil outdoor experience. Best hiking trails around Maylis The most popular hiking route is Maylis to Doazit via l'étang de la Hosse – loop, a 13.5 miles (21.7 km) trail that takes 5 hours 46 minutes to complete, passing by the Étang de la Hosse. Another top favourite among local hikers is The Chalosse Viewpoint loop from Nerbis, an easy 3.6 miles (5.7 km) path offering views over the Chalosse region. Local hikers also love the Brassempouy Prehistoric Site loop from Brassempouy, a 4.4 miles (7.1 km) trail leading through the area around the Brassempouy Prehistoric Site, often completed in about 1 hour 58 minutes. Hiking around Maylis is defined by rural paths, gentle hills, and local points of interest like the Étang de la Hosse. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Maylis are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 190 hikers have used komoot to explore Maylis's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking around Maylis?
A: Hiking around Maylis offers a gently undulating landscape with relatively low elevations, typically ranging from 43 to 122 meters. The trails often traverse rural areas, agricultural lands, and may include small forests or ponds, providing a tranquil outdoor experience rather than challenging mountainous terrain.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Maylis?
A: There are nearly 30 hiking routes available around Maylis, catering to various preferences and skill levels. These trails have been explored by over 190 hikers using komoot.

Q: Are there hiking trails for all skill levels in Maylis?
A: Yes, Maylis offers a range of hiking trails suitable for different abilities. You can find 12 easy routes for leisurely walks, 14 moderate trails for those seeking a bit more challenge, and 3 difficult routes for experienced hikers.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Maylis?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24835226" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Chalosse Viewpoint loop from Nerbis&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.7 km path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41535494" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brassempouy Prehistoric Site loop from Brassempouy&lt;/a&gt; is a 7.1 km easy trail.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see on hikes around Maylis?
A: Hikes around Maylis often feature rural paths and local points of interest. You might encounter the Étang de la Hosse, a notable pond mentioned in some routes. Additionally, you can find viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5789369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Chalosse Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6095647" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Montfort-en-Chalosse viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic vistas of the region.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Maylis?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 80 reviews. Hikers often praise the tranquil outdoor experience, the gently undulating landscape, and the variety of routes available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Maylis?
A: Yes, Maylis offers several easy routes that are suitable for families. Trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24835226" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Chalosse Viewpoint loop from Nerbis&lt;/a&gt; (5.7 km) or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37622533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Chalosse Viewpoint loop from Mugron&lt;/a&gt; (2.8 km) are good choices for a pleasant outing with children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Maylis?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on trails in Maylis are not detailed, generally, dogs are welcome on many rural paths in France, especially if kept on a leash. It's always advisable to ensure your dog is well-behaved and to clean up after them. Check local signage for any specific restrictions.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Maylis?
A: Given the gently undulating terrain and lack of high-altitude challenges, hiking in Maylis is generally pleasant during spring, summer, and autumn. Spring offers blooming landscapes, while autumn provides comfortable temperatures and colorful scenery. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Maylis?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For routes starting directly from Maylis or nearby villages, you can often find parking in the village centers or designated areas. For specific routes, it's recommended to check the route details on komoot for information on starting points and potential parking spots.

Q: Are there any cafes or pubs near the hiking trails in Maylis?
A: Maylis is a small commune, so options directly on the trails might be limited. However, you can find local establishments in Maylis itself or in nearby villages like Nerbis, Mugron, or Brassempouy, which serve as starting points for some routes. These places often offer opportunities for refreshments or a meal after your hike.

Q: What is the longest hiking trail available in Maylis?
A: One of the longer and more challenging routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996605114/maylis-to-doazit-via-l-etang-de-la-hosse-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maylis to Doazit via l'étang de la Hosse – loop&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 21.7 km and is classified as difficult. It typically takes around 5 hours 46 minutes to complete.
tips: A small pond with a big impact. Arrive at the right time, with the sun on your shoulders, and enjoy all its glory.
